layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: THREE-COLUMN COMPARISON TABLE layout with a full-width title at the top, followed by a unified comparison table spanning the full slide width. The table is structured as three equal-width columns (Criteria, Athena, Best-of-breed) with a fourth narrow column for cost data. All columns share identical row heights, creating a strict horizontal alignment grid. A full-width footer bar at the bottom contains strategic takeaway text. The layout uses generous padding on all sides, with dashed grey separator lines between rows to create visual rhythm without consuming vertical space. The design follows the Flat Geometry Table pattern (Section 4 Option C) for formal decision-making content.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 4
  Allocation: [0.15, 0.3, 0.43, 0.12]

Containers:
  C1 (SLIDE TITLE): Full-width title area at the top of the slide, containing the recall statement and decision summary. Text uses heading font, brand primary color, left-aligned. A visible gap separates this title from the comparison table below. No background fill, no border. Height is compact — approximately 12% of slide height, just enough to contain the two-line title text with minimal padding above and below.
  C0 (CRITERIA COLUMN (leftmost)): Leftmost column of the comparison table, containing row labels for the five evaluation criteria (Speed to market, Accuracy, IP ownership, Long-term fungibility & viability, Total cost of ownership). Column width is approximately 15% of slide width — narrow, sized to fit the longest label text without wrapping. Each criterion label sits in its own row section with equal flexGrow values across all five rows, ensuring uniform row heights. Labels use body font, bold, brand dark color, left-aligned with a small left inset, vertically centered within each row. Light grey background fill on all label cells. Thin dashed grey horizontal separator lines between rows (not after the last row). No column header — a transparent spacer at the top matches the header height of adjacent columns.
  C2 (ATHENA COLUMN (second from left)): Second column of the comparison table, presenting the Athena platform evaluation. Column width is approximately 30% of slide width. Column header at top: bold text 'Athena', brand dark color, left-aligned, followed immediately by a small accent tab (brand primary fill, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned beneath the header text), then a thin solid horizontal line (brand dark color) spanning the full column width and touching the bottom edge of the accent tab. Five row sections below the header, each with equal flexGrow matching the Criteria column rows. Each row contains 2–3 bullet points using native PowerPoint bullets (brand color bullet markers), body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset. Bullets describe Athena's characteristics for that criterion. Thin dashed grey horizontal separator lines between rows at identical vertical positions as the Criteria column separators. White background for all data rows.
  C3 (BEST-OF-BREED COLUMN (third from left)): Third column of the comparison table, presenting the Best-of-breed platform evaluation. Column width is approximately 43% of slide width — the widest column, reflecting the greater content volume. Column header at top: bold text 'Best-of-breed', brand dark color, left-aligned, followed by the same accent tab + solid line treatment as the Athena column (accent tab: brand primary fill, very thin, narrow, top corners rounded, left-aligned; solid line: brand dark color, full column width, touching the tab). Light blue background fill on the entire column (very light tint, not saturated — signals the recommended path visually). Five row sections below the header, each with equal flexGrow matching the other columns' rows. Each row contains 2–3 bullet points using native PowerPoint bullets (brand color bullet markers), body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset. Bullets describe Best-of-breed characteristics for that criterion. Thin dashed grey horizontal separator lines between rows at identical vertical positions as the other columns.
  C4 (COST DATA ROW (bottom section of table)): Full-width bottom row of the comparison table, spanning all four columns horizontally. This row presents the Total cost of ownership criterion. Height is approximately 8% of slide height — slightly taller than the rows above to accommodate the denser cost breakdown text. The row is divided into the same four-column structure: leftmost cell (15% width) contains the 'Total cost of ownership' label (light grey fill, bold text, left-aligned, vertically centered); second cell (30% width) contains Athena cost bullets (white background, 2 bullets: higher ongoing run-rate cost, lower upfront build cost, with dollar values in parentheses); third cell (43% width) contains Best-of-breed cost bullets (light blue background matching C3, 3 bullets: lower ongoing run-rate cost with detailed breakdown, higher upfront build cost, dollar values in parentheses). Light pink/salmon background fill on the Athena and Best-of-breed cost cells to visually distinguish cost data from the criteria rows above. A thin dashed grey horizontal separator line sits above this row, separating it from the Long-term fungibility row.
  C5 (FOOTER BAR (strategic takeaway)): Full-width footer bar at the very bottom of the slide, below the comparison table. Height is compact — approximately 8% of slide height, just enough to contain two lines of text with small top/bottom padding. Fill: light brand accent color (soft green tint). No border. No rounded corners (flat strip for formal tone). Contains two bullet points in body font, brand dark color, left-aligned with a small left inset: (1) 'The strategic platform decision must align strongly to business priority, valuing quality, precision, and extensibility above speed-to-market as a standalone metric' and (2) 'A custom Azure agentic framework optimizes for sustained accuracy, adaptability, and long-term economic and strategic resilience'. Text uses native PowerPoint bullets with brand-color bullet markers. A small footnote reference '1. In addition, Graphine...' sits at the bottom-left corner of the slide in very small body font, grey color, outside the footer bar.
